item.title: Iran says obstacles remain, but acknowledges deal taking shape with U.S.
item.description: As the Trump administration signals hope for a possible deal, Iran says obstacles linger, and "frequent changes" in U.S. positions aren't helping.

item.title: Trump approves emergency declaration for California chemical leak, Newsom says
item.description: California crews conducted an overnight operation at the Orange County site of a chemical leak in Garden Grove and said Monday that the threat of a massive explosion had been "eliminated."

item.title: Honoring fallen U.S. service members on Memorial Day
item.description: In celebrations throughout the country, communities honored the lives of fallen U.S. service members. Jericka Duncan takes a look at the history of Memorial Day.

item.title: Remembering CBS News' Paul Douglas and James Brolan 20 years after their deaths
item.description: On this Memorial Day, we remember CBS News cameraman Paul Douglas and soundman James Brolan, who were killed 20 years ago by a car bomb in Iraq.

item.title: Inside the U.S. military's war games using AI
item.description: The U.S. military has been using artificial intelligence to process battlefield intelligence in real time. Chris Livesay observes a U.S. military training exercise in Morocco that's being shaped by AI.

item.title: Here's what the draft memo for a proposed deal with Iran includes
item.description: A draft memorandum includes a 60-day ceasefire extension and the halt of fighting on all fronts, sources say.

item.title: AG requests to resume ballroom construction, citing latest shooting
item.description: Attorney General Todd Blanche said the ballroom is "being constructed for the physical safety and security of all Presidents, their families, staff, Foreign Dignitaries, and guests."

item.title: High gas prices and storms hamper Memorial Day travel weekend
item.description: Millions of drivers hit the road for a record-breaking Memorial Day travel weekend despite high gas prices. Meanwhile, three days of relentless rain in the northeast U.S. hampered travel plans. Skyler Henry has more.

item.title: Shooting near White House raises security concerns ahead of America's 250th birthday celebrations
item.description: Saturday's shooting near the White House has raised security concerns ahead of summer celebrations to mark America's 250th birthday. The shooter, who was killed after opening fire on a Secret Service checkpoint, had previously blocked a White House entry lane last June, court records show. Nicole Sganga reports.

item.title: What to know about the chemical tank in California that forced evacuations
item.description: The tank at GKN Aerospace is estimated to contain 7,000 gallons of methyl methacrylate, a volatile chemical used to produce plastics.

item.title: Residents near California chemical tank forced to flee: "You're freaking out"
item.description: Emergency crews raced overnight to prevent a tank holding a volatile industrial chemical from exploding at an aerospace facility in Southern California.

item.title: Risk of catastrophic blast from California chemical leak eliminated, but other concerns remain
item.description: The threat of a catastrophic explosion from a chemical vapor leak in Orange County, California, was ruled out Monday after officials discovered a crack in a failing tank at GKN Aerospace Saturday night. Concerns of a smaller explosion still remain. Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

item.title: Latest on U.S.-Iran peace talks as Trump signals deal could be close
item.description: President Trump said negotiations with Iran were "proceeding nicely," although no deal has been formalized by Monday night. CBS News' Nikole Killion and Imtiaz Tyab have the latest.

item.title: Where do Israel's leaders stand on possible U.S.-Iran peace deal?
item.description: Israeli leaders have been largely watching the peace talks between the U.S. and Iran from the sidelines. As negotiators meet, the Israeli military continues to strike Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, including on Monday. Imtiaz Tyab has more.

item.title: Trump says he will either sign "great and meaningful" deal with Iran or no deal at all
item.description: The marathon peace talks between the U.S. and Iran appear to have no clear finish line. Over the weekend, President Trump signaled an agreement could be close. But by Monday night, that appears even less certain. Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Wander Franco found criminally responsible for abusing minor, avoids jail time
item.description: A Dominican judge has ruled that Tampa Bay Rays shortstop Wander Franco is criminally responsible for the sexual and psychological abuse of a minor but will not serve a sentence.

item.title: What are the sticking points for U.S. and Iran peace negotiations?
item.description: Lawmakers reacted after news of a potential breakthrough in peace talks between the U.S. and Iran emerged over the weekend. CBS News' Taureen Small has the latest on lawmakers' reaction, and retired Navy Vice Adm. Robert Murrett joins to discuss the potential deal.

item.title: Where the U.S.-Iran peace deal stands as Trump says negotiations are "proceeding nicely"
item.description: The Trump administration signaled progress on peace talks with Iran. A draft for a potential agreement includes the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and a commitment by Iran to never develop a nuclear weapon, CBS News has learned. CBS News' Nikole Killion and Imtiaz Tyab have more.

item.title: 1,500 strangers attend WWII veteran's funeral
item.description: Veterans service officer Terrance O'Keefe put out a call from Massachusetts for a World War II veteran who needed a proper hero's send-off. When he arrived at the funeral, a line was already out the church door.

item.title: Latest news on U.S.-Iran peace deal process as Trump, Tehran provide details
item.description: There appears to be progress in talks between the U.S. and Iran, but the process toward a peace deal may still take some time. CBS News' Aaron MacLean has more insight.

item.title: Threat of massive blast eliminated for California chemical leak, officials say
item.description: More than 50,000 people in California remain evacuated due to an Orange County chemical leak that prompted Gov. Newsom to request that President Trump issue a Federal Emergency Declaration.

item.title: Slowed U.S.-Iran peace deal progress may be due to Supreme Leader's Mojtaba Khamenei location
item.description: Iran's supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is holed up in an undisclosed location with little access to the outside world, U.S. officials tell CBS News. This comes as news of some progress in peace talks emerges. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab reports.

item.title: Pope Leo calls for "disarming" of AI in technology-focused encyclical
item.description: In his "Magnifica Humanitas" encyclical, Pope Leo warns that as civilization grapples with the power of AI, the main challenge is remaining "profoundly human."

item.title: DOJ says it scrubbed news releases about Jan. 6 criminal cases from its website
item.description: The Justice Department acknowledges it has removed from its website news releases about criminal cases related to the Jan. 6, 2021 riot, calling the information "partisan propaganda."

item.title: Husband convicted of hiring hitman to kill NYC art dealer in Brazil
item.description: Daniel Sikkema faces a mandatory sentence of life in prison. Brent Sikkema was stabbed to death in his Rio de Janeiro townhouse in 2024.

item.title: American Music Awards air tonight with star-studded performances
item.description: The American Music Awards celebrate fan favorites in the music world and feature performances from multiple artists.

item.title: What's open and closed for Memorial Day 2026?
item.description: Most retail stores will be open for business on Memorial Day, while post offices will be closed. Here's what to know.

item.title: 5/24: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as President Trump says a peace deal with Iran has been "largely negotiated," Imtiaz Tyab reports from Tel Aviv and Sen. Chris Van Hollen discusses the possible deal. Plus, on this Memorial Day weekend, Medal of Honor recipients retired Lt. Col. William Swenson and retired Command Sergeant Major Matthew Williams join.

item.title: Over 40,000 evacuated in California chemical leak as officials warn tank may explode
item.description: Southern California officials have expanded evacuations in several Orange County cities due to concerns over a leaking toxic chemical tank that they say is "actively in crisis."

item.title: 5/17: Sunday Morning "By Design"
item.description: Jane Pauley hosts our annual look at design. Featured: Tiny homes in your backyard; Louis Vuitton; Adobe house construction; Finland's Marimekko; domino art; Philadelphia food favorites; chandeliers; Longwood Gardens; rare maps; and director Jon Favreau of "The Mandalorian and Grogu."
